description: Strep-tag II — an engineered 8-residue synthetic affinity peptide (WSHPQFEK) that binds the biotin pocket of streptavidin / engineered streptavidin for mild, single-step affinity purification and detection.

Use of the Strep-Tag and streptavidin for detection and purification of recombinant proteins
Improved affinity of engineered streptavidin for the Strep-tag II peptide is due to a fixed open conformation of the lid-like loop at the binding site
The Strep-tag system for one-step purification and high-affinity detection or capturing of proteins

label: Strep_tag
note: Strep-tag II core affinity peptide, WSHPQFEK (8 aa). Synthetic engineered peptide that binds the biotin-binding pocket of streptavidin and of engineered high-affinity streptavidin (Strep-Tactin) in a 3-10-helical conformation; eluted competitively by desthiobiotin or biotin. Atomic part: the 8 residues are the functional unit and carry no internal functional sub-elements. Verified to 100% identity over all 8 residues against PDB 1RSU chain P (positions 3-10).

provenance.sequence_source: 1RSU_P (PDB 1RSU chain P, "STREP-TAG II PEPTIDE", SNWSHPQFEK, 10 aa; the 8-aa Strep-tag II core WSHPQFEK = positions 3-10, 100% identity over full query length; deposited 1995-10-19, released 1997-05-15; Schmidt TG, Koepke J, Frank R, Skerra A; J Mol Biol 255:753-766, 1996; PMID 8636976; DOI 10.1006/jmbi.1996.0061)

functional_claims.label: The peptide binds the biotin-binding pocket of streptavidin, adopting a 3-10-helical conformation; the Glu7/Lys9 (His-Pro-Gln-Phe-Glu-Lys) substitutions relative to the original Strep-tag optimise this streptavidin-pocket binding mode.

value.mechanism: binds the biotin-binding pocket of streptavidin

source.quote: Molecular interaction between the Strep-tag affinity peptide and its cognate target, streptavidin

functional_claims.label: Genetic fusion of the Strep-tag II peptide to a recombinant protein permits one-step affinity purification on immobilised streptavidin (or engineered streptavidin), with gentle competitive elution by desthiobiotin or biotin under physiological conditions.

source.quote: The Strep-tag system for one-step purification and high-affinity detection or capturing of proteins

functional_claims.label: The Strep-tag II peptide binds streptavidin with a dissociation constant of approximately 1 uM; affinity-engineered streptavidin (Strep-Tactin) raises this affinity by roughly an order of magnitude.

source.quote: Use of the Strep-Tag and streptavidin for detection and purification of recombinant proteins

functional_claims.label: Host- and position-agnostic: the small synthetic peptide can be fused at either the N- or C-terminus and functions for purification and detection across diverse expression hosts, including bacterial and mammalian cell-culture systems.

source.quote: Applications of a peptide ligand for streptavidin: the Strep-tag

functional_claims.label: Strep-tag II (WSHPQFEK) is the rationally engineered successor to the original library-selected Strep-tag (AWRHPQFGG); a tandem avidity version, the Twin-Strep-tag, links two WSHPQFEK copies via a GGGSGGGSGGS linker for higher-affinity (near-pM) capture.

source.quote: Development of the Twin-Strep-tag and its application for purification of recombinant proteins from cell culture supernatants
